title: Crocodile Watches the Pieces Move
description: On pages 7–10, this part focuses on crocodile watches the pieces move, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Crocodile Watches the Pieces Move. Crocodile defeats Luffy and leaves him to die, while Vivi and the crew race toward the capital without their captain. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: where the hell did he disap- pear to...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Damn that Mr. Prince...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I swear I'll find you,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Shuffle shuffle

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And kill you on the spot...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Drag...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Aghhhh!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: We're gonna die! We're gonna die!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: It's all over for us!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The water's already up to our knees!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Hey, you dumb crocodile! Over here!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: W-What's wrong with you, Nami!?

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: I'm trying to anger it so it'll bite and break open this cage for us!

dialogue.speaker: Tashigi
dialogue.text: G-Good idea!

dialogue.speaker: Tashigi
dialogue.text: Over here, you big fat banana!

dialogue.speaker: Tashigi
dialogue.text: Luffy, I told you that's wrong! It's the banana that's growing from the crocodile, which is why its name hassle" at the end.

dialogue.speaker: Tashigi
dialogue.text: For example, the word "Monkey Pance" refers to a dance even though the word "monkey" is an animal...

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: What's the difference? Both bananas and crocodiles are something you eat.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's coming!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Kyaa!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Aghhh!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: It's no good!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Crumble,

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: What the hell is this cage made out of!?

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: GAR:Ghhh!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Hurry up and save us, VIVI!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hey, you...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: And why the hell are you so damn calm right now!?

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Help us think of a way to escape!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: How much do you know,

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: About what Crocodile intends to achieve...?

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: That woman beside Crocodile... She's a wanted woman who's been on the run from the World Government for over 20 years...

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: 0-Over seventy Million!?

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: S-S-SO WHAT!?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: From the moment those two joined hands, their plan turned into something far more than taking over a mere country.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: If left alone, this might develop into an incident large enough to engulf the whole world!

summary: This part develops crocodile watches the pieces move and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
